evals.name: full-sweep
evals.prompt:
Fix all issues:

```dockerfile
FROM node:latest
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN npm install
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl
ENV API_KEY=sk-secret-1234567890
ENV DATABASE_URL=postgres://admin:password@db:5432/myapp
EXPOSE 3000
CMD node dist/server.js
```

```yaml
# docker-compose.yml
services:
  app:
    build:
      context: .
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      - .:/app
    environment:
      - API_KEY=sk-secret-1234567890
      - NODE_ENV=production
    depends_on:
      - db
      - redis

  db:
    image: postgres:latest
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: admin
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password
      POSTGRES_DB: myapp

  redis:
    image: redis:latest
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
```

Output fixed code only.

assertions.trap: FROM node:latest uses :latest tag
assertions.description: Must pin exact base image versions, never use :latest. E.g. node:22-alpine3.20

assertions.trap: Single FROM stage with no multi-stage separation
assertions.description: Must use multi-stage builds: deps -> build -> dev -> production stages

assertions.trap: COPY . . before npm install busts cache on every code change
assertions.description: COPY package.json package-lock.json first, then npm ci, THEN COPY source

assertions.trap: RUN npm install instead of npm ci
assertions.description: Use npm ci (lockfile-based) not npm install in Docker for reproducible builds

assertions.trap: OS deps (apt-get) installed after COPY . . instead of before
assertions.description: Order layers by change frequency: OS deps > lockfile > install > source > build

assertions.trap: CMD node dist/server.js uses shell form instead of exec form
assertions.description: Always use exec form ["..."] for CMD/ENTRYPOINT, never shell form (breaks signal handling)

assertions.trap: No USER directive, runs as root
assertions.description: Must create and switch to non-root USER in production stage

assertions.trap: ENV API_KEY=sk-secret-1234567890 bakes secret into image layer
assertions.description: Never put secrets in Dockerfile ENV/COPY. Use runtime env vars or mounted secrets

assertions.trap: API_KEY=sk-secret-1234567890 hardcoded in compose environment
assertions.description: Never hardcode secrets in compose file. Use env_file: .env with .env in .gitignore

assertions.trap: No HEALTHCHECK instruction in Dockerfile
assertions.description: Production stage must include HEALTHCHECK instruction

assertions.trap: FROM node:latest uses full image instead of alpine/slim variant
assertions.description: Use Alpine for small images, or -slim if native deps have musl issues

assertions.trap: No NODE_ENV=production in production Dockerfile stage
assertions.description: Set ENV NODE_ENV=production in the production stage

assertions.trap: No tini or dumb-init for PID 1 signal forwarding
assertions.description: Use tini or dumb-init as ENTRYPOINT for proper signal handling and zombie reaping

assertions.trap: Bind mount .:/app overwrites container node_modules with no anonymous volume
assertions.description: Use anonymous volume /app/node_modules to prevent bind mount from overwriting container deps

assertions.trap: depends_on uses list form without condition: service_healthy
assertions.description: Use depends_on with condition: service_healthy, not just container start

assertions.trap: db and redis services have no healthcheck defined
assertions.description: Healthchecks on every service in compose for proper dependency ordering

assertions.trap: postgres:latest and redis:latest use :latest tag
assertions.description: Pin exact image versions in compose, never :latest

assertions.trap: db ports: 5432:5432 exposes to all interfaces instead of 127.0.0.1
assertions.description: Bind dev-only ports to 127.0.0.1 to prevent external access

assertions.trap: db has no named volume for /var/lib/postgresql/data, data lost on restart
assertions.description: Use named volumes for persistent data (postgres, redis). Containers are ephemeral

assertions.trap: No security_opt: no-new-privileges on app service
assertions.description: Set security_opt: [no-new-privileges:true] to prevent privilege escalation

assertions.trap: No cap_drop: [ALL] on services
assertions.description: Drop all capabilities with cap_drop: [ALL], then add back only what is needed

assertions.trap: No deploy.resources.limits on any service
assertions.description: Set deploy.resources.limits (memory, cpus) on every production service

assertions.trap: No LABEL org.opencontainers.image.* metadata in Dockerfile
assertions.description: Add OCI labels for traceability: source, version, revision

assertions.trap: RUN npm install without --mount=type=cache for npm cache
assertions.description: Use cache mounts for package managers: RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm ci

assertions.trap: Separate RUN for npm install and apt-get creates unnecessary layers
assertions.description: Combine related commands with && to reduce layers while keeping readability

assertions.trap: No ENTRYPOINT defined, CMD used alone for binary and args
assertions.description: Use ENTRYPOINT for the binary and CMD for default args, allowing runtime override

assertions.trap: No read_only: true with tmpfs on app service
assertions.description: Use read_only: true with explicit tmpfs for writable dirs to minimize attack surface

assertions.trap: No --chown on COPY means files owned by root
assertions.description: Use COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup to ensure copied files are owned by non-root user

assertions.trap: redis has no volume for /data, data lost on restart
assertions.description: Redis needs a named volume for /data to persist data across restarts

assertions.trap: build has no target specified, builds entire Dockerfile
assertions.description: Specify build target (e.g. target: dev) to build only the intended stage
